test_patch:
diff --git a/tests/test_linkshort_boundaries.py b/tests/test_linkshort_boundaries.py
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..83c2335
--- /dev/null
+++ b/tests/test_linkshort_boundaries.py
@@ -0,0 +1,216 @@
+"""Boundary / edge-case correctness for the link-shortener backend.
+
+These assert correct outputs on inputs the visible HTTP suite never feeds:
+a settings CSV whose middle element is whitespace-only, an ``Authorization:
+Bearer`` header whose token must not gain a leading space, a timezone-aware
+timestamp at a non-UTC offset that must be *converted* (not relabelled) to
+UTC, the first page of an owner's link list, and a database URL whose query
+string happens to contain a second ``postgresql://`` literal. The existing
+tests drive values comfortably away from these edges, so a boundary
+regression here stays invisible to them.
+"""
+
+from __future__ import annotations
+
+import unittest
+from datetime import datetime, timedelta, timezone
+from types import SimpleNamespace
+
+
+def _fresh_session():
+    """An isolated in-memory SQLite session with the Link table created.
+
+    Mirrors tests/conftest.py: a single shared in-memory connection so the
+    schema created here is visible to the session under test.
+    """
+    from sqlalchemy import create_engine
+    from sqlalchemy.orm import sessionmaker
+    from sqlalchemy.pool import StaticPool
+
+    from link_shortener.database import Base
+    from link_shortener import models  # noqa: F401  (registers the Link table)
+
+    engine = create_engine(
+        "sqlite://",
+        connect_args={"check_same_thread": False},
+        poolclass=StaticPool,
+    )
+    Base.metadata.create_all(bind=engine)
+    return sessionmaker(bind=engine, autoflush=False, autocommit=False)()
+
+
+def _seed_links(session, owner_id: str, count: int):
+    """Insert ``count`` links for ``owner_id`` with strictly increasing
+    created_at, so ``codeN`` is newer than ``code(N-1)``. Returns the codes
+    in insertion (oldest-first) order."""
+    from link_shortener.models import Link
+
+    base = datetime(2024, 1, 1, 12, 0, tzinfo=timezone.utc)
+    codes = []
+    for i in range(count):
+        code = f"code{i}"
+        session.add(
+            Link(
+                code=code,
+                original_url=f"https://example.com/{i}",
+                created_at=base + timedelta(minutes=i),
+                owner_id=owner_id,
+            )
+        )
+        codes.append(code)
+    session.commit()
+    return codes
+
+
+# ── Defect 1: config.parse_authorized_parties ─────────────────────────────────
+class TestAuthorizedPartiesParsing(unittest.TestCase):
+    def test_whitespace_only_element_is_filtered(self) -> None:
+        from link_shortener.config import Settings
+
+        # "a, ,b" -> ["a", " ", "b"]; the whitespace-only middle element is not
+        # a real party and must be dropped, not kept as an empty string.
+        settings = Settings(database_url="sqlite://", clerk_authorized_parties="a, ,b")
+        self.assertEqual(settings.clerk_authorized_parties, ["a", "b"])
+
+    # p2p — behaviour that stays correct across the fix
+    def test_plain_csv_is_split_and_trimmed(self) -> None:
+        from link_shortener.config import Settings
+
+        settings = Settings(
+            database_url="sqlite://", clerk_authorized_parties="x.com, y.com"
+        )
+        self.assertEqual(settings.clerk_authorized_parties, ["x.com", "y.com"])
+
+    def test_list_value_passes_through(self) -> None:
+        from link_shortener.config import Settings
+
+        settings = Settings(
+            database_url="sqlite://", clerk_authorized_parties=["a", "b"]
+        )
+        self.assertEqual(settings.clerk_authorized_parties, ["a", "b"])
+
+    def test_sibling_cors_parser_filters_whitespace_only(self) -> None:
+        # The adjacent CORS parser handles the same CSV shape correctly; the two
+        # parsers should agree on whitespace-only elements.
+        from link_shortener.config import Settings
+
+        settings = Settings(database_url="sqlite://", cors_allowed_origins="a, ,b")
+        self.assertEqual(settings.cors_allowed_origins, ["a", "b"])
+
+
+# ── Defect 2: main._rate_limit_key ────────────────────────────────────────────
+class TestRateLimitKey(unittest.TestCase):
+    def test_bearer_token_key_has_no_leading_space(self) -> None:
+        from link_shortener.main import _rate_limit_key
+
+        req = SimpleNamespace(headers={"Authorization": "Bearer TOK"})
+        self.assertEqual(_rate_limit_key(req), "user:TOK")
+
+    # p2p — anonymous callers fall back to the remote address, unchanged
+    def test_anonymous_request_uses_remote_address(self) -> None:
+        from link_shortener.main import _rate_limit_key
+
+        req = SimpleNamespace(headers={}, client=SimpleNamespace(host="203.0.113.7"))
+        self.assertEqual(_rate_limit_key(req), "203.0.113.7")
+
+
+# ── Defect 3: services._as_utc ────────────────────────────────────────────────
+class TestAsUtc(unittest.TestCase):
+    def test_aware_offset_is_converted_to_utc(self) -> None:
+        from link_shortener.services import _as_utc
+
+        # 14:00 at +05:30 is 08:30 UTC; the offset must be applied, not dropped.
+        value = datetime(2024, 1, 1, 14, 0, tzinfo=timezone(timedelta(hours=5, minutes=30)))
+        result = _as_utc(value)
+        self.assertEqual(result.utcoffset(), timedelta(0))
+        self.assertEqual((result.hour, result.minute), (8, 30))
+        self.assertEqual(result, datetime(2024, 1, 1, 8, 30, tzinfo=timezone.utc))
+
+    # p2p — naive / None / already-UTC inputs stay correct across the fix
+    def test_naive_input_is_stamped_utc(self) -> None:
+        from link_shortener.services import _as_utc
+
+        result = _as_utc(datetime(2024, 1, 1, 12, 0))
+        self.assertEqual(result, datetime(2024, 1, 1, 12, 0, tzinfo=timezone.utc))
+
+    def test_none_returns_none(self) -> None:
+        from link_shortener.services import _as_utc
+
+        self.assertIsNone(_as_utc(None))
+
+    def test_utc_input_is_unchanged(self) -> None:
+        from link_shortener.services import _as_utc
+
+        value = datetime(2024, 1, 1, 10, 0, tzinfo=timezone.utc)
+        self.assertEqual(_as_utc(value), datetime(2024, 1, 1, 10, 0, tzinfo=timezone.utc))
+
+
+# ── Defect 4: services.list_links (pagination offset) ─────────────────────────
+class TestListLinksPagination(unittest.TestCase):
+    def test_first_page_returns_newest_rows(self) -> None:
+        from link_shortener.services import list_links
+
+        session = _fresh_session()
+        try:
+            _seed_links(session, "owner_a", 5)  # code0 (oldest) .. code4 (newest)
+            page = list_links(session, page=1, page_size=2, owner_id="owner_a")
+            self.assertEqual([link.code for link in page], ["code4", "code3"])
+        finally:
+            session.close()
+
+    # p2p — count and empty-owner behaviour are independent of the offset math
+    def test_count_links_reports_total(self) -> None:
+        from link_shortener.services import count_links
+
+        session = _fresh_session()
+        try:
+            _seed_links(session, "owner_a", 5)
+            self.assertEqual(count_links(session, owner_id="owner_a"), 5)
+        finally:
+            session.close()
+
+    def test_owner_with_no_links_returns_empty(self) -> None:
+        from link_shortener.services import list_links
+
+        session = _fresh_session()
+        try:
+            _seed_links(session, "owner_a", 5)
+            page = list_links(session, page=1, page_size=2, owner_id="nobody")
+            self.assertEqual(page, [])
+        finally:
+            session.close()
+
+
+# ── Defect 5: config.prefer_psycopg_driver ────────────────────────────────────
+class TestPreferPsycopgDriver(unittest.TestCase):
+    def test_only_the_scheme_prefix_is_rewritten(self) -> None:
+        from link_shortener.config import Settings
+
+        url = "postgresql://u@h/d?cb=postgresql://x"
+        settings = Settings(database_url=url)
+        self.assertEqual(
+            settings.database_url, "postgresql+psycopg://u@h/d?cb=postgresql://x"
+        )
+
+    # p2p — single-occurrence, non-postgres, and already-psycopg URLs stay correct
+    def test_plain_postgres_scheme_is_rewritten(self) -> None:
+        from link_shortener.config import Settings
+
+        settings = Settings(database_url="postgresql://u@h/d")
+        self.assertEqual(settings.database_url, "postgresql+psycopg://u@h/d")
+
+    def test_non_postgres_url_is_untouched(self) -> None:
+        from link_shortener.config import Settings
+
+        settings = Settings(database_url="sqlite://")
+        self.assertEqual(settings.database_url, "sqlite://")
+
+    def test_already_psycopg_url_is_untouched(self) -> None:
+        from link_shortener.config import Settings
+
+        settings = Settings(database_url="postgresql+psycopg://u@h/d")
+        self.assertEqual(settings.database_url, "postgresql+psycopg://u@h/d")
+
+
+if __name__ == "__main__":
+    unittest.main()
